login.description: Authenticates a user and returns an access token.

me.description: Retrieves the current authenticated user's details.

get_by_id.description: Retrieves a single item by its ID.

modules.main.py: Application entry point, FastAPI app instance

schemas/.user.py: Pydantic models for User (UserCreate, UserResponse, Token)
schemas/.item.py: Pydantic models for Item (ItemCreate, ItemResponse, ItemUpdate)

crud/.user.py: CRUD operations for User model (database interaction)
crud/.item.py: CRUD operations for Item model (database interaction)

models/.user.py: SQLAlchemy ORM model for User table
models/.item.py: SQLAlchemy ORM model for Item table

dependencies/.auth.py: Dependency for JWT token verification and current user retrieval

core/.security.py: Password hashing, JWT token creation/verification utilities

migrations.name: create_users_table
migrations.content:
CREATE TABLE users (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    email VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    hashed_password VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

migrations.name: create_items_table
migrations.content:
CREATE TABLE items (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    title VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    description TEXT,
    owner_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

  "api_code": {
    "main.py": "from fastapi import FastAPI
from routers import auth, items

app = FastAPI()

app.include_router(auth.router)
app.include_router(items.router)",
    "routers/auth.py": "from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException
from fastapi.security import OAuth2PasswordRequestForm
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session
from typing import Annotated

from schemas import user as user_schema
from crud import user as user_crud
from dependencies import database, auth as auth_dependency
from core import security

router = APIRouter(prefix='/auth', tags=['auth'])

@router.post('/signup', response_model=user_schema.UserResponse)
async def signup(user: user_schema.UserCreate, db: Session = Depends(database.get_db)):
    db_user = user_crud.get_user_by_email(db, email=user.email)
    if db_user:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail='Email already registered')
    hashed_password = security.get_password_hash(user.password)
    return user_crud.create_user(db=db, user=user, hashed_password=hashed_password)

@router.post('/login')
async def login(form_data: OAuth2PasswordRequestForm = Depends(), db: Session = Depends(database.get_db)):
    user = auth_dependency.authenticate_user(db, form_data.username, form_data.password)
    if not user:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail='Incorrect email or password')
    access_token = security.create_access_token(data={'sub': user.email})
    return {'access_token': access_token, 'token_type': 'bearer'}

@router.get('/me', response_model=user_schema.UserResponse)
async def read_users_me(current_user: Annotated[user_schema.UserResponse, Depends(auth_dependency.get_current_user)]) -> user_schema.UserResponse:
    return current_user",
    "routers/items.py": "from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session
from typing import Annotated, List

from schemas import item as item_schema
from crud import item as item_crud
from dependencies import database, auth as auth_dependency
from schemas import user as user_schema

router = APIRouter(prefix='/items', tags=['items'])

@router.post('/', response_model=item_schema.ItemResponse)
async def create_item(item: item_schema.ItemCreate, current_user: Annotated[user_schema.UserResponse, Depends(auth_dependency.get_current_user)], db: Session = Depends(database.get_db)):
    return item_crud.create_item(db=db, item=item, user_id=current_user.id)

@router.get('/', response_model=List[item_schema.ItemResponse])
async def read_items(skip: int = 0, limit: int = 100, current_user: Annotated[user_schema.UserResponse, Depends(auth_dependency.get_current_user)], db: Session = Depends(database.get_db)):
    items = item_crud.get_items(db, skip=skip, limit=limit, user_id=current_user.id)
    return items

@router.get('/{item_id}', response_model=item_schema.ItemResponse)
async def read_item(item_id: str, current_user: Annotated[user_schema.UserResponse, Depends(auth_dependency.get_current_user)], db: Session = Depends(database.get_db)):
    db_item = item_crud.get_item(db, item_id=item_id, user_id=current_user.id)
    if db_item is None:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail='Item not found')
    return db_item

@router.put('/{item_id}', response_model=item_schema.ItemResponse)
async def update_item(item_id: str, item: item_schema.ItemUpdate, current_user: Annotated[user_schema.UserResponse, Depends(auth_dependency.get_current_user)], db: Session = Depends(database.get_db)):
    db_item = item_crud.get_item(db, item_id=item_id, user_id=current_user.id)
    if db_item is None:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail='Item not found')

    updated_item = item_crud.update_item(db, item_id=item_id, item=item, user_id=current_user.id)
    return updated_item

@router.delete('/{item_id}')
async def delete_item(item_id: str, current_user: Annotated[user_schema.UserResponse, Depends(auth_dependency.get_current_user)], db: Session = Depends(database.get_db)):
    db_item = item_crud.get_item(db, item_id=item_id, user_id=current_user.id)
    if db_item is None:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail='Item not found')
    item_crud.delete_item(db, item_id=item_id, user_id=current_user.id)
    return {'message': 'Item deleted successfully'}",
    "schemas/user.py": "from pydantic import BaseModel
from uuid import UUID

class UserBase(BaseModel):
    email: str

class UserCreate(UserBase):
    password: str

class UserResponse(UserBase):
    id: UUID

    class Config:
        orm_mode = True

class Token(BaseModel):
    access_token: str
    token_type: str",
    "schemas/item.py": "from pydantic import BaseModel
from uuid import UUID
from typing import Optional

class ItemBase(BaseModel):
    title: str
    description: Optional[str] = None

class ItemCreate(ItemBase):
    pass

class ItemUpdate(ItemBase):
    title: Optional[str] = None
    description: Optional[str] = None

class ItemResponse(ItemBase):
    id: UUID
    owner_id: UUID

    class Config:
        orm_mode = True",
    "crud/user.py": "from sqlalchemy.orm import Session

from models import user as user_model
from schemas import user as user_schema

def get_user(db: Session, user_id: str):
    return db.query(user_model.User).filter(user_model.User.id == user_id).first()


def get_user_by_email(db: Session, email: str):
    return db.query(user_model.User).filter(user_model.User.email == email).first()


def get_users(db: Session, skip: int = 0, limit: int = 100):
    return db.query(user_model.User).offset(skip).limit(limit).all()


def create_user(db: Session, user: user_schema.UserCreate, hashed_password: str):
    db_user = user_model.User(email=user.email, hashed_password=hashed_password)
    db.add(db_user)
    db.commit()
    db.refresh(db_user)
    return db_user",
    "crud/item.py": "from sqlalchemy.orm import Session
from typing import List

from models import item as item_model
from schemas import item as item_schema


def get_item(db: Session, item_id: str, user_id: str):
    return db.query(item_model.Item).filter(item_model.Item.id == item_id, item_model.Item.owner_id == user_id).first()


def get_items(db: Session, skip: int = 0, limit: int = 100, user_id: str) -> List[item_model.Item]:
    return db.query(item_model.Item).filter(item_model.Item.owner_id == user_id).offset(skip).limit(limit).all()


def create_item(db: Session, item: item_schema.ItemCreate, user_id: str):
    db_item = item_model.Item(**item.dict(), owner_id=user_id)
    db.add(db_item)
    db.commit()
    db.refresh(db_item)
    return db_item

def update_item(db: Session, item_id: str, item: item_schema.ItemUpdate, user_id: str):
    db_item = db.query(item_model.Item).filter(item_model.Item.id == item_id, item_model.Item.owner_id == user_id).first()

    if db_item is None:
        return None

    for key, value in item.dict(exclude_unset=True).items():
        setattr(db_item, key, value)

    db.add(db_item)
    db.commit()
    db.refresh(db_item)
    return db_item

def delete_item(db: Session, item_id: str, user_id: str):
    db_item = db.query(item_model.Item).filter(item_model.Item.id == item_id, item_model.Item.owner_id == user_id).first()
    db.delete(db_item)
    db.commit()",
    "models/user.py": "from sqlalchemy import Column, String, DateTime
from sqlalchemy.dialects.postgresql import UUID
from sqlalchemy.sql import func
from sqlalchemy.orm import relationship

from database import Base
from .item import Item

class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'users'

    id = Column(UUID(as_uuid=True), primary_key=True, default=func.gen_random_uuid())
    email = Column(String, unique=True, nullable=False)
    hashed_password = Column(String, nullable=False)
    created_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now())
    updated_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now(), onupdate=func.now())

    items = relationship('Item', back_populates='owner')
",
    "models/item.py": "from sqlalchemy import Column, String, ForeignKey, DateTime
from sqlalchemy.dialects.postgresql import UUID
from sqlalchemy.sql import func
from sqlalchemy.orm import relationship

from database import Base


class Item(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'items'

    id = Column(UUID(as_uuid=True), primary_key=True, default=func.gen_random_uuid())
    title = Column(String, nullable=False)
    description = Column(String)
    owner_id = Column(UUID(as_uuid=True), ForeignKey('users.id', ondelete='CASCADE'), nullable=False)
    created_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now())
    updated_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now(), onupdate=func.now())

    owner = relationship('User', back_populates='items')
",

  "integration_tests": {

      "test_auth_integration.py": "import pytest
from httpx import AsyncClient
from sqlalchemy import create_engine
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker
from sqlalchemy.pool import StaticPool
from fastapi import FastAPI
from fastapi.routing import APIRoute

from database import Base
from main import app
from dependencies import database
from core.config import settings

SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URL = 'sqlite:///:memory:'

engine = create_engine(
    SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URL, connect_args={'check_same_thread': False}, poolclass=StaticPool
)
TestingSessionLocal = sessionmaker(autocommit=False, autoflush=False, bind=engine)


def override_get_db():
    try:
        db = TestingSessionLocal()
        yield db
    finally:
        db.close()


app.dependency_overrides[database.get_db] = override_get_db

@pytest.fixture(scope='session', autouse=True)
async def create_test_database():
    Base.metadata.create_all(engine)
    yield
    Base.metadata.drop_all(engine)

@pytest.fixture
async def async_client():
    async with AsyncClient(app=app, base_url='http://test') as client:
        yield client

async def create_user(client: AsyncClient, email, password):
  response = await client.post('/auth/signup', json={'email': email, 'password': password})
  return response.json(), response.status_code

async def login_user(client: AsyncClient, email, password):
  form_data = {'username': email, 'password': password}
  response = await client.post('/auth/login', data=form_data)
  return response.json(), response.status_code


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_signup_login_me(async_client: AsyncClient):
  # Signup
  signup_data, signup_status = await create_user(async_client, 'test@example.com', 'password123')
  assert signup_status == 200
  assert signup_data['email'] == 'test@example.com'
  assert 'id' in signup_data

  # Login
  login_data, login_status = await login_user(async_client, 'test@example.com', 'password123')
  assert login_status == 200
  assert 'access_token' in login_data
  assert login_data['token_type'] == 'bearer'

  access_token = login_data['access_token']
  async_client.headers['Authorization'] = f'Bearer {access_token}'

  # Me
  me_response = await async_client.get('/auth/me')
  me_data = me_response.json()
  me_status = me_response.status_code

  assert me_status == 200
  assert me_data['email'] == 'test@example.com'
  assert me_data['id'] == signup_data['id']

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_signup_duplicate_email(async_client: AsyncClient):
  # Signup
  await create_user(async_client, 'test2@example.com', 'password123')

  # Signup with the same email
  signup_data, signup_status = await create_user(async_client, 'test2@example.com', 'password456')
  assert signup_status == 400
  assert signup_data['detail'] == 'Email already registered'

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_login_incorrect_password(async_client: AsyncClient):
  # Signup
  await create_user(async_client, 'test3@example.com', 'password123')

  # Login with incorrect password
  login_data, login_status = await login_user(async_client, 'test3@example.com', 'wrong_password')
  assert login_status == 400
  assert login_data['detail'] == 'Incorrect email or password'

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_login_nonexistent_user(async_client: AsyncClient):
  # Login with nonexistent user
  login_data, login_status = await login_user(async_client, 'nonexistent@example.com', 'password123')
  assert login_status == 400
  assert login_data['detail'] == 'Incorrect email or password'",
      "test_items_integration.py": "import pytest
import uuid
from httpx import AsyncClient
from sqlalchemy import create_engine
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker
from sqlalchemy.pool import StaticPool
from fastapi import FastAPI

from database import Base
from main import app
from dependencies import database
from core.config import settings

SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URL = 'sqlite:///:memory:'

engine = create_engine(
    SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URL, connect_args={'check_same_thread': False}, poolclass=StaticPool
)
TestingSessionLocal = sessionmaker(autocommit=False, autoflush=False, bind=engine)


def override_get_db():
    try:
        db = TestingSessionLocal()
        yield db
    finally:
        db.close()


app.dependency_overrides[database.get_db] = override_get_db

@pytest.fixture(scope='session', autouse=True)
async def create_test_database():
    Base.metadata.create_all(engine)
    yield
    Base.metadata.drop_all(engine)

@pytest.fixture
async def async_client():
    async with AsyncClient(app=app, base_url='http://test') as client:
        yield client

async def create_user(client: AsyncClient, email, password):
  response = await client.post('/auth/signup', json={'email': email, 'password': password})
  return response.json(), response.status_code

async def login_user(client: AsyncClient, email, password):
  form_data = {'username': email, 'password': password}
  response = await client.post('/auth/login', data=form_data)
  return response.json(), response.status_code

async def create_item(client: AsyncClient, access_token, title, description):
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {access_token}'}
    response = await client.post('/items', headers=headers, json={'title': title, 'description': description})
    return response.json(), response.status_code

async def get_items(client: AsyncClient, access_token):
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {access_token}'}
    response = await client.get('/items', headers=headers)
    return response.json(), response.status_code

async def get_item_by_id(client: AsyncClient, access_token, item_id):
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {access_token}'}
    response = await client.get(f'/items/{item_id}', headers=headers)
    return response.json(), response.status_code

async def update_item(client: AsyncClient, access_token, item_id, title=None, description=None):
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {access_token}'}
    payload = {}
    if title: payload['title'] = title
    if description: payload['description'] = description
    response = await client.put(f'/items/{item_id}', headers=headers, json=payload)
    return response.json(), response.status_code

async def delete_item(client: AsyncClient, access_token, item_id):
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {access_token}'}
    response = await client.delete(f'/items/{item_id}', headers=headers)
    return response.json(), response.status_code

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_create_get_update_delete_item(async_client: AsyncClient):
    # Signup and Login
    signup_data, signup_status = await create_user(async_client, 'itemtest@example.com', 'password123')
    assert signup_status == 200
    login_data, login_status = await login_user(async_client, 'itemtest@example.com', 'password123')
    assert login_status == 200
    access_token = login_data['access_token']

    # Create Item
    item_data, item_status = await create_item(async_client, access_token, 'Test Item', 'Test Description')
    assert item_status == 200
    assert item_data['title'] == 'Test Item'
    assert item_data['description'] == 'Test Description'
    item_id = item_data['id']

    # Get Items
    items_data, items_status = await get_items(async_client, access_token)
    assert items_status == 200
    assert len(items_data) > 0
    assert items_data[0]['title'] == 'Test Item'

    # Get Item by ID
    item_by_id_data, item_by_id_status = await get_item_by_id(async_client, access_token, item_id)
    assert item_by_id_status == 200
    assert item_by_id_data['title'] == 'Test Item'
    assert item_by_id_data['description'] == 'Test Description'

    # Update Item
    updated_item_data, updated_item_status = await update_item(async_client, access_token, item_id, title='Updated Item', description='Updated Description')
    assert updated_item_status == 200
    assert updated_item_data['title'] == 'Updated Item'
    assert updated_item_data['description'] == 'Updated Description'

    # Get Item by ID after update
    item_by_id_data, item_by_id_status = await get_item_by_id(async_client, access_token, item_id)
    assert item_by_id_status == 200
    assert item_by_id_data['title'] == 'Updated Item'
    assert item_by_id_data['description'] == 'Updated Description'

    # Delete Item
    delete_item_data, delete_item_status = await delete_item(async_client, access_token, item_id)
    assert delete_item_status == 200
    assert delete_item_data['message'] == 'Item deleted successfully'

    # Get Item by ID after deletion
    item_by_id_data, item_by_id_status = await get_item_by_id(async_client, access_token, item_id)
    assert item_by_id_status == 404"
    },
